card.lead: TBD (owner of H2 2025 TH Marketing Plan, name not in deck)
card.budget: Per H2 2025 plan (figures not disclosed in deck)
card.lastTouchNote: Received H2 2025 marketing plan deck, 60 pages
card.situation: WF entered TH mid-2025, already captured 1.1% market share. THB launch shipped July 2025 (so onshore in TH now). Total TH B2B cross-border payment market: $80B (2025), 7.1% CAGR. Banks (SCB 21%, KBank, BBL) hold ~62% (down from 70%); fintechs ~45% combined. WISE is #4 (~8.9%), Payoneer ~6.7%, Airwallex/PingPong/LianLian smaller. WF scorecard: lowest in Market Share (1/5), Product Awareness (2/5), User Understanding (2/5); strongest in SME features, E-com/ERP integration, API, Scalability, Card Support, Contract Flexibility, Product Breadth (all 5/5). THB Support / Local Payout sits at 3 (parity with global fintechs, weaker than local banks).
asks: Identify TH marketing lead (who owns this plan day-to-day) and add to regions card
Cross-check H2 2025 Influencer Affiliate Program execution status — was it launched? what NFC/CPA actuals?
Verify TH localization status: blog ('early-stage' in plan), Thai social ('just launched'), Thai site is live
Compare TH influencer plan against SG (Joshua) UGC playbook and VN (Thao) freelancer playbook — TH has the most developed influencer affiliate program in the SEA cohort; harvest learnings
Sprout Social access for TH lead once identified
next: Identify TH lead and schedule sync
Pull TH content/campaign performance from current state (Sprout, internal dashboards) to compare against H2 2025 plan targets
Map TH plan's 5-region playbook to current calendar / production pipeline
Confirm TH plan is still active for 2026 or a refresh is in flight
benchmarks: TH B2B cross-border payment market: $80B (2025), 7.1% CAGR
Banks dominant: SCB 21%, KBank 17.8%, BBL 23.3% combined ~62%
Competitor annual marketing spend: SCB 80M THB, BBL 70M, KBank 60M, Wise 7M, PingPong 5M, Aspire 4.5M, Payoneer 5M, Airwallex 3M, LianLian 3M
SMEs: 3.7M total (84.7% Micro / 13.6% Small / 1.7% Medium); ~48% of trade value
Influencer Affiliate reward: ฿1,000-2,000 per NFC + tiered bonuses (10 NFC = +฿3,000), iPhone for top performer

products.name: Pay to China
products.problem: Thai SMEs face delays and high FX fees when importing from China
products.solution: Pay directly to 1688.com and Chinese suppliers in CNY through local Chinese rails
products.impact: Save 2-5% on FX & transaction fees, same-day settlement, eliminate sourcing agents

products.name: World Card
products.tagline: No FX Fees + Cashback on Global Business Spend
products.problem: Thai businesses pay 2-3.5% extra for foreign expenses (ads, subscriptions, travel)
products.solution: Multi-currency business card with no FX fees and up to 1% cashback
products.impact: Reduce cost for marketing, earn cashback on global spend, real-time control and report

regionalPlaybook.campaign: Factory Floors to Global Stores: Digital Growth Hub
regionalPlaybook.concept: Position WorldFirst as the premier partner for digitally mature SMEs aiming to expand internationally
activities: Strategic partnerships with platforms like Shopify

regionalPlaybook.provinces: Khon Kaen, Nakhon Ratchasima, Ubon Ratchathani
regionalPlaybook.industries: Agriculture, food processing, silk, OTOP, social commerce
regionalPlaybook.campaign: Local Goods to Global Hoods: Isan SMEs Go Inter
regionalPlaybook.concept: Encourage local SMEs to explore international markets via relatable success stories and educational resources

regionalPlaybook.industries: Handicrafts, herbal products, agro-processing, boutique exports
regionalPlaybook.campaign: Crafted Hands to Worldwide Brands: Crafting Global Connections with Creativity
regionalPlaybook.concept: Highlight the region's unique crafts and products, positioning WorldFirst as the bridge to global markets

regionalPlaybook.industries: Tourism, seafood processing, border trade (Malaysia), seasonal retail
regionalPlaybook.campaign: Seaside Fields to Border Bills: Southern SMEs Sailing Internationally
regionalPlaybook.concept: Leverage region's maritime and tourism strengths to promote int'l trade opportunities

regionalPlaybook.campaign: Assembly Lines to Tech Heights: Industrial Evolution, Eastern SMEs in the Global Arena
regionalPlaybook.concept: Support industrial SMEs in EEC to expand global footprint

communicationInsights.industryExpertImpact: 77% of SMEs influenced by trusted industry-expert endorsements
communicationInsights.communityInfluence: 85% of provider choice influenced by community
communicationInsights.linePresence: 5M LINE OA accounts in TH, 90% are SMEs
communicationInsights.firstMoverGap: Few competitors engage Thai KOLs/KOCs in B2B cross-border — first-mover advantage

pillars.name: Community Advocate
pillars.audience: Cross-border SMEs (B2B & B2C), E-com sellers, Brand.com

pillars.name: Paid Acquisition
pillars.channels: Google + Meta (MQL: registration/CPL/NFC), Adtech partnerships (Kerry Express parcel media, BTS Group data partnership)

pillars.name: Local Initiative
activities: Event sponsorships at local trade fairs

competitorCommStrategy.name: SCB
competitorCommStrategy.strategy: Professional, reassuring, solution-oriented. Biz continuity + global reach
competitorCommStrategy.valueProp: All-in-one biz banking, FX, payroll, 24/7 app
competitorCommStrategy.media: Digital ads, social, PR, events, print, TV, search, YouTube

competitorCommStrategy.name: KBank
competitorCommStrategy.strategy: Upbeat, SME-focused, casual online + pro PR
competitorCommStrategy.valueProp: Free-fee digital biz platform, high transfer limits, payroll
competitorCommStrategy.media: TV, search, digital, webinars, influencer, social, community events

competitorCommStrategy.name: Bangkok Bank
competitorCommStrategy.valueProp: Full treasury/FX, SWIFT, BAHTNET, multi-currency deposits
competitorCommStrategy.media: TV, print, economic forums, LinkedIn, limited digital

competitorCommStrategy.name: Wise
competitorCommStrategy.valueProp: Multi-currency account, low FX, mid-market rate
competitorCommStrategy.media: Search, blogs, social, PR, SEO, no traditional

competitorCommStrategy.name: Payoneer
competitorCommStrategy.strategy: Aspirational, professional. Promotes global biz growth for freelancers/SMEs
competitorCommStrategy.valueProp: Marketplace payouts, USD/EUR/GBP setup, fast TH withdrawals
competitorCommStrategy.media: Social ads, webinars, YouTube, blogs, Amazon partnerships

competitorCommStrategy.name: Airwallex
competitorCommStrategy.valueProp: Financial OS, FX, global biz account, multi-layer controls
competitorCommStrategy.media: OOH, search/social, fintech expos, digital PR, video

competitorCommStrategy.name: PingPong
competitorCommStrategy.valueProp: Cross-border payout accounts for e-com, audit trail, treasury control
competitorCommStrategy.media: Google Ads, LinkedIn, webinars, B2B expos, limited traditional

competitorCommStrategy.name: LianLian Pay
competitorCommStrategy.strategy: Formal, service-oriented, global reach + low-cost ops
competitorCommStrategy.valueProp: Multi-currency, rapid supplier payments, PromptPay refunds
competitorCommStrategy.media: Trade portal banners, Facebook, FedEx co-marketing, digital-only

competitorCommStrategy.name: Aspire
competitorCommStrategy.strategy: Modern, tech-savvy, appeals to startups/finance teams
competitorCommStrategy.valueProp: Corporate cards, multi-currency, expense automation, startup-ready APIs
competitorCommStrategy.media: Search, blog, LinkedIn, startup hubs, co-working events, referrals
